Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 10:06 ET
Bullish flow contradicts bearish regime as CPI looms in 3 days
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Flow composite at +37 clashes with regime composite at -21, creating 58-point divergence amid negative gamma amplification
  • 2. Index puts heavy at 1.59 P/C while single-names show 1.04 P/C, signaling institutional hedging against individual stock selection
  • 3. CPI in 3 days with negative $6B SPY gamma creates explosive two-way risk into the print
NARRATIVE — bearish regime bullish flow

The most striking signal today is the massive flow-regime divergence, with bullish positioning (+37 flow composite) building against a bearish macro backdrop (-21 regime composite). This 58-point split occurs as SPY sits in negative gamma territory at -$6.1B, meaning dealer hedging will amplify any directional moves into Wednesday's CPI print. The VIX at 24.7 with CNN Fear & Greed at just 22 suggests institutional caution despite the underlying bullish flow. Massive block activity in SPY reveals positioning for volatility around the 657-658 strike zone, with 51K calls at 657 and 37K calls at 658 competing against 35K puts at 656. The QQQ April 10th 515 puts — 15,000 contracts at just 9 cents — represents cheap downside protection nearly 13% out-of-the-money. This aligns with the index P/C divergence, where institutions are loading puts on broad indices (1.59 P/C) while maintaining more balanced exposure on individual names (1.04 P/C). Individual stock volatility divergences highlight the stock-picking environment, with implied correlation at just 0.19 creating dispersion opportunities. Ten names trade in backwardation including AAPL, AMZN, and META, while technical momentum favors MRVL (87.2 score) and AMD (77.7) against bearish setups in TSLA (20.3) and MSFT (22.1). The vol-rich names like DASH (+212% vs sector) suggest single-name event risk is being priced aggressively relative to index protection.

Layer 1: Sentiment +14Layer 2: GEX -$6.1BLayer 3: P/C divergenceLayer 9: CPI 3d
